name: Hong Kong
intro: Hong Kong's project roles concentrate where the money is: banks and insurers running regulatory and digital programmes, the exchange ecosystem, and regional headquarters managing Greater Bay Area initiatives. Delivery jobs here are demanding and well paid, and hiring managers — often serving global reporting lines — use PMP and Scrum certifications as immediate proof of method. In a market where roles move fast, the credential keeps CVs in play. Our live online weekend batch runs on the IST schedule, which converts to civilised late-morning to evening hours in Hong Kong. You train with an international cohort in English, matching the territory's working style. Invoices suit corporate sponsorship. Afterwards we guide the exam application, run full-length mock exams, and stay available for questions until you clear the exam.

faq.q: How much does certification training cost in Hong Kong?
faq.a: The course fee is US$1,295 for PMP and US$695 for CSM, covering the full live online weekend workshop, study materials, mock exams and exam application support. PMI's PMP exam fee (US$425 member / US$675 non-member) is paid separately to PMI; the CSM exam is included in the course fee.

faq.q: Are the classes live or recorded?
faq.a: Every batch is taught live by an instructor over two weekend days — not pre-recorded videos. You can ask questions in real time, work through exercises with the cohort, and reach the trainer afterwards for doubts.

faq.q: Is the certification recognised by employers in Hong Kong?
faq.a: Yes. PMP (from PMI) and CSM (from Scrum Alliance) are global credentials recognised identically everywhere, including by employers and recruiters across Hong Kong.
